description: A generic log-rotation or cleanup job deletes compliance evidence before its mandated retention period elapses.
body: Failure mode: a default retention or cost-saving cleanup purges logs, approvals, or attestations after a short window, but those artifacts were the evidence a control framework requires to be kept for years, so an assessment finds the period uncovered. Detection signals: evidence for older periods is missing; retention is governed by a generic rotation policy unaware of compliance classes; no lifecycle lock prevents early deletion. Do not apply one blanket retention to both transient logs and compliance evidence. Safer fallback: tag each evidence class with its required window, apply a lifecycle policy that blocks deletion until it elapses, and reconcile against privacy limits so personal data is not over-retained either.
